title: The End of the Tunnel — Case File 4: The Heidelberg Escape
description: Case File 4 — Detective: The fourth chapter turns the investigation toward the Heidelberg side, renovation routes, and forged artifacts.

answerKey.method: An electrical trap staged as a renovation accident in the Heidelberg service lift
answerKey.motive: To hide that renovation routes were being used to move forged artifacts and blackmail files
answerKey.keyEvidence: the cut ground wire, tile dust on Rhett’s boots, and the altered work order
answerKey.explanation: Rhett Boudreaux is responsible. the cut ground wire, tile dust on Rhett’s boots, and the altered work order proves the method and timeline. This chapter reveals: The famous escape route was being rebuilt as a modern smuggling path.

publicClues.title: Case File 4: The Heidelberg Escape
publicClues.text: Case File 4: The Heidelberg Escape begins inside The Tunnel. This is one chapter in a larger story, not a separate remake of the same mystery.

publicClues.title: The New Death
publicClues.text: The victim is Bennett Clay. The old Peacock Alley legend still matters, but this chapter adds a deeper layer to the ongoing investigation.

phone.title: Phone Evidence 1
phone.text: Bennett called Rhett after finding a fresh work order under an old wall panel.

phone.title: Phone Evidence 2
phone.text: Rhett’s phone was off for seventeen minutes during the service-lift failure.

messages.title: Messages Evidence 1
messages.text: Bennett texted: “The artifacts are not coming in through the bar.”

messages.title: Messages Evidence 2
messages.text: Rhett replied: “Stay out of the service lift unless you want to get hurt.”

maps.title: Maps Evidence 1
maps.text: Rhett’s device appears below the Heidelberg stairwell before the lift alarm.

maps.title: Maps Evidence 2
maps.text: Bennett’s phone drops signal exactly where the work order says no one should be.

bank.title: Bank Evidence 1
bank.text: A contractor payment is split into three deposits under different restoration labels.

bank.title: Bank Evidence 2
bank.text: One deposit references the same artifact lot Bennett was investigating.

photos.title: Photos Evidence 1
photos.text: A photo shows fresh tile dust on Rhett’s boot soles.

photos.title: Photos Evidence 2
photos.text: Another shows the lift panel screws removed before the official maintenance window.

social.title: Social Evidence 1
social.text: A collector forum post advertises “verified tunnel-era artifacts” with Bennett tagged.

social.title: Social Evidence 2
social.text: The listing disappears after Bennett challenges the provenance.

contacts.title: Contacts Evidence 2
contacts.text: A note says: “He does not forge papers. He moves the boxes.”

notes.title: Notes Evidence 1
notes.text: Detective note: Rhett’s motive is not history. It is transportation.

notes.title: Notes Evidence 2
notes.text: Detective note: the altered work order is a map of where the operation moved.

files.title: Files Evidence 1
files.text: The work-order PDF was modified after Bennett requested a copy.

files.title: Files Evidence 2
files.text: The original version routes workers through a sealed corridor that later disappears from the file.

browser.title: Browser Evidence 1
browser.text: A search asks how to disable a service-lift ground wire without tripping the main breaker.

browser.title: Browser Evidence 2
browser.text: Another search asks how to remove tile dust from rubber boot treads.

openingNarration: Detectives, welcome to Case File 4: The Heidelberg Escape. Julian’s access route led investigators toward the Heidelberg service lift. Collector Bennett Clay is found dead in what looks like a renovation accident. This chapter is harder because the tunnel is now a logistics map, not just a crime scene.
revealScript: Rhett Boudreaux killed Bennett Clay with a staged electrical trap because Bennett discovered the renovation path was being used to move forged artifacts and blackmail files. The Heidelberg escape was no longer a legend. It was infrastructure.

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 1: What proves Bennett’s death was staged?

options.label: A detail that sounds dramatic but does not prove the case
The service lift had a cut ground wire before the maintenance window
A rumor from the old hotel story with no physical support
A coincidence that does not affect the timeline
